content:
## Overview
## Architecture

### Workspace Model (D46)

A workspace is a purpose-built directory. Target repos are NOT polluted with Corvia artifacts.

```
my-workspace/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ corvia.toml              ‚Üê has [workspace] section
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .corvia/                 ‚Üê knowledge store, indexes, coordination
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .git/                ‚Üê knowledge git (source of truth)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ knowledge/           ‚Üê git-tracked JSON knowledge files
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ hnsw_index/          ‚Üê LiteStore vector index
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ coordination.redb
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ .gitignore
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ repos/                   ‚Üê cloned or symlinked target repos
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ repo-a/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ repo-b/
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ .devcontainer/           ‚Üê optional
```


### Config Format (D47)

Unified `corvia.toml` with optional `[workspace]` section (Cargo-style):

```toml
[project]
name = "my-project"
scope_id = "my-org"

[workspace]
repos_dir = "repos"

[[workspace.repos]]
name = "backend"
url = "https://github.com/org/backend"
local = "/home/dev/backend"           # optional local override
namespace = "backend"

[[workspace.repos]]
name = "frontend"
url = "https://github.com/org/frontend"
namespace = "frontend"

[storage]
store_type = "lite"
data_dir = ".corvia"

[embedding]
provider = "ollama"
model = "nomic-embed-text"
url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434"
dimensions = 768

[server]
host = "127.0.0.1"
port = 8020
```

**Detection**: If `corvia.toml` contains `[workspace]`, the directory is a workspace. If not, backward-compatible single-project mode.

**Namespace mapping**: Each repo's `namespace` field maps to the `workstream` segment: `{org}:{scope_id}:{namespace}:{source}:{version_ref}`.

**Local overrides**: If `local` path exists and is a valid git repo, use it directly (no clone). Otherwise clone from `url` into `{repos_dir}/{name}`.
